title: oxytropis middendorffii subsp. middendorffii
intro: Oxytropis middendorffii subsp. middendorffii, also known as Middendorff's oxytropis, is a perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of Siberia and the Russian Far East. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Middendorff's oxytropis are believed to include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and improving digestion, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its roots and leaves can be used in various forms, including powder and extract.
